name: Suraj Gola
role: Backend Developer | Competitive Programmer | Open Source Contributor
bio: First-year B.Tech CSE student with a strong focus on backend systems, API design, and competitive programming. Currently building scalable backend projects, contributing to open source, and sharpening problem-solving skills with the goal of becoming a backend engineer at a top product company. Hackathon winner with hands-on experience in real-world system design and AI-integrated applications.
objective: To grow as a backend engineer by building robust, scalable systems and APIs, contributing to meaningful open source projects, and solving challenging problems. Actively working toward securing a backend engineering role at a product-focused company where I can create real impact. In the short term, I am focused on strengthening my backend development skills, mastering system design principles, and excelling in competitive programming to prepare for technical interviews at top product companies.

projects.title: CareMatrix
projects.description: Engineered an AI-powered healthcare backend system for real-time patient monitoring and clinical decision support. Designed and exposed REST APIs using FastAPI, integrated AI models for health insights, and managed structured data pipelines — improving healthcare management efficiency end-to-end.

projects.title: Smart Yatra
projects.description: Built a backend-driven smart travel platform handling trip planning and route optimization logic. Focused on clean API design and efficient structured data handling to deliver a seamless user experience across travel workflows.

projects.title: Repo Scanning System
projects.description: Developed an automated repository scanning tool that detects security vulnerabilities and code inefficiencies across codebases. Designed as an open source utility to help teams enforce code quality and safety standards at scale.

achievements.title: Research Paper Publication
achievements.description: Presented a research paper at 32nd International Conference at AKS University Satna, on the topic AI Guided Design of Eco-Friendly Coatings from Food Waste which will be published in the conference proceedings book with an ISBN Number.

certificates.title: Winner @ CodeSphere Hackathon – ACIC Incubation Program at SGT University

sections.title: Summary
sections.content: First-year B.Tech CSE student with a strong foundation in backend development, REST API design, and automation. Competitive programmer actively solving DSA problems and contributing to open source. Hackathon winner with experience building and shipping real-world backend systems. Targeting backend engineering roles at product-focused companies.

sections.title: Key Achievements
sections.content:
• Winner - CodeSphere Hackathon (ACIC Incubation Program, SGT University)
• Finalist - RIFT '26 PW Hackathon
• Selected for ACIC Incubation Program at SGT University
• Published research paper in reputed conference/book
• Active open source contributor on GitHub
• Consistent competitive programmer on LeetCode / Codeforces
